rules.condition: [Patch rule — dermatology_7class] Dominant color is a single, uniform reddish-brown or dark-brown hue with no discrete multicolor zones (no focal black, blue-grey, or white areas); Overall shape is roughly symmetric and the border, while slightly irregular, is soft and fades gradually rather than showing abrupt notching or radial streaks/pseudopods; Absence of blue-white veil (no confluent blue-grey opacity overlying pigment); Absence of regression structures (no white scar-like depigmented patches or peppering of grey granules); Absence of atypical vascular structures (no dotted, irregular, or polymorphous vessels visible at lesion periphery); No discrete satellite nodules or focal areas of raised, structureless black pigment; Pigment network, if visible, is regular and symmetric — with evenly spaced, uniform grid lines and holes of consistent size — not broadened, irregularly thickened, or abruptly ending at the periphery; No irregularly distributed dots or globules (any dots or globules present are uniform in size, color, and distributed symmetrically throughout the lesion, consistent with a regular globular or combined nevus pattern); Lesion has a flat or minimally elevated surface texture without focal nodular elevation, ulceration, or surface erosion; Overall lesion diameter is consistent with a benign nevus context (not markedly large or rapidly expanding in apparent surface area, as inferred from lesion morphology); No shiny white streaks (chrysalis structures) visible under polarized light, which would raise suspicion for melanoma or other malignancy; The lesion is a single, discrete, well-defined melanocytic unit without evidence of confluence with adjacent separate lesions
rules.action: Classify as Melanocytic Nevus. Confidence: moderate. Rule: When a lesion shows a diffuse, homogeneous reddish-brown to dark-brown pigmentation with a roughly symmetric, well-circumscribed but softly bordered outline, no focal areas of regression (white scar-like zones), no blue-white veil, no atypical pigment network with irregular streaks or pseudopods, and the overall color variation is limited to a single dominant hue (uniform reddish-brown) without discrete zones of black, blue-grey, or white, classify as Melanocytic Nevus. [CONTRA: Melanoma]
